mv4: Unite and empower students in healthcare by equipping them with the knowledge, skills, and values to become competent, culturally aware healthcare workers and global health professionals, working towards equal access to healthcare and achieving health for all.

_opmerking voor editors: Vanaf hier, heb je de vertalingen voor bemsagent.html
bg3: BeMSA Ghent is the Ghent faculty branch of the Belgian Medical Students’ Association (BeMSA), a student-run organisation that represents medical students across 11 faculties in Belgium. As part of this national network and the
bg4: Each year, BeMSA Ghent organises a wide range of local, national, and international activities, including clinical and research exchanges that allow students to explore different healthcare systems, medical innovations, and approaches to healthcare delivery.
bg5: BeMSA Ghent brings students together to exchange ideas, collaborate, and develop initiatives aimed at creating a healthier world. It empowers its members by providing training, skills, and opportunities to grow into future health leaders. Through advocacy, projects, and campaigns, BeMSA Ghent actively contributes to addressing pressing health issues and making a positive impact on both medical students and the communities they serve.
bg6: , BeMSA is also connected to a global community of 137 national member organisations.
bg7: International Federation of Medical Students’ Associations (IFMSA)
bg9: BeMSA Ghent grew out of SWISS (Student Working Group on International Cooperation and Internships), a working group focused on the combination of “medical students and international experience,” both in a practical, informational, and awareness-oriented way.
bg10: In 2009, medical students from Ghent (SWISS), Antwerp, and Leuven joined forces and founded the national organisation BeMSA (Belgian Medical Students’ Association), which became a candidate member of the International Federation of Medical Students’ Associations (IFMSA).
bg11: This opened the door to a global network of contacts and unique opportunities. From the very beginning, we chose to be part of the BeMSA story and therefore adopted the name BeMSA Ghent.
bg12: To discover more about the history of BeMSA Ghent, press the button below to explore our timeline.
bg15: Through the election of national board members and seats in the General Assembly, we contribute to the development of BeMSA’s vision and positions. These national positions have an impact at the international level through the presence of Ghent delegates from BeMSA at international IFMSA meetings.
bg17: In IFMSA and BeMSA (as described on IFMSA and BeMSA), the Standing Committees are the core working structures through which medical students actively engage in different global health topics.
bg18: There are 7 Standing Committees, each focusing on a specific area such as public health, medical education, research, exchanges, human rights, and sexual and reproductive health. Within each committee, students develop and run local, national, and international projects, campaigns, trainings, and exchanges, allowing them to gain experience, build skills, and contribute to meaningful change in healthcare and medical education.
bg19: In BeMSA Ghent, these committees are implemented through local project groups, giving students the opportunity to participate in initiatives that match their interests while being part of a wider national and international network. Each Standing Committee represents a different way for students to take action and grow as future health professionals.
bg20: Each Standing Committee represents a different way for students to take action and grow as future health professionals.
bg21: Press the logo below to discover more about each Standing Committee and their projects.
bg23: BeMSA Ghent's board is what turns all the student's ideas into reality. The BeMSA Ghent board, which is elected each year, consists of the following functions:
bg28: Meet our current board and project coordinators and learn about our past boards through the buttons below.

pv1: A Project Coordinator ensures that BeMSA Ghent projects are well organized and run smoothly. They oversee the planning and management of projects, assign tasks to team members, coordinate communication, and keep track of progress. They also contact sponsors and external partners to support project activities and make sure everything stays on schedule and in good hands.

_opmerking voor editors: Vanaf hier, heb je de vertalingen voor gog.html
gog1: Dear board members, project leaders, volunteers, and participants in our BeMSA Ghent projects, Unfortunately, inappropriate and boundary-crossing behavior still exists in our society. In response to this, the board of BeMSA Ghent has created a platform where victims of inappropriate behavior can share their story, either anonymously or non-anonymously. With this message, we want to emphasize that misconduct of any kind will not be tolerated within BeMSA Ghent. We strive to create a safe and open environment where everyone can be themselves without fear or compromise. This reporting platform is open to all students participating in our projects (more information is available via the button below).
gog2: Reporting Point for Inappropriate and Transgressive Behavior
gog3: Additionally, we would like to point out external resources that are not affiliated with BeMSA but may be helpful in situations involving misconduct:
gog4: Additionally, we have also included several websites and chat forums where you can seek help for general problems:
gog5: Remember that you are never alone when taking on these challenges.

howtosponsorbemsagentnpo: How to sponsor BeMSA Ghent npo?
sup1: Sponsors are very important for BeMSA, as they determine the extent to which we can implement our health projects. Below are our general sponsorship packages. However, we are very flexible and always willing to do everything possible to reach an agreement from which both you and we can benefit as much as possible.
sup3: Website: sponsors page and a separate page with information and links
sup4: Posters, invitations, and flyers for all activities
sup6: Banner in the BeMSA Ghent meeting room
sup11: Any form of promotional material (T-shirts, caps, drinks, food products, discount vouchers, personal care products, keychains, pens, notepads, magazines, etc.) is gratefully accepted and, in consultation with you, converted into a tailored form of publicity.
sup12: For more information about BeMSA, you can always consult our press kit:
sup14: Affordable combination packages can be put together. Contact our PR team for a customized quotation.
